sourceMovie.title: Always
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Always for the same reason it broke through in 2011: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Song Il-gon keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The romance and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hitch
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Always clicked for you, Hitch (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Purple Hearts
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Always clicked for you, Purple Hearts (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Always?
faq.answer: Try Hitch, Purple Hearts, What Dreams May Come—each shares DNA with Always while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Always?
faq.answer: Always (2011) is best described as Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Always worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
